title: Jun 2–6 Weekly Market Report
report:
## Market Overview (Jun 2–6)

42 of 67 themes (63%) posted gains, with the average theme change at +1.45%, a significant improvement from +0.82% the prior week. Semiconductor and AI themes rallied broadly, driving the overall market higher.

The Nikkei 225 gained +2.3% for the week, reclaiming the 39,000 level. Strong earnings guidance from NVIDIA and accelerating AI infrastructure investment concentrated capital flows into the semiconductor sector. USD/JPY edged up to 156.2 (+0.5%), supporting export-oriented themes.

---

## Top Rising Themes

### 🥇 1st: MLCC / Electronic Components (+8.2%)

Top performer for the second straight week. Murata Manufacturing (6981) surpassed ¥14 trillion in market cap and continued trading at all-time highs. Taiyo Yuden (6976) extended its rally on the back of a +535% YoY net income increase. The structural demand thesis — AI servers requiring 3–5x the MLCC count of conventional servers — continues to drive strong buying interest. Volume surged +35% WoW, signaling clear institutional inflows.

**Key stocks**: Murata (6981) +10.1%, Taiyo Yuden (6976) +7.8%, TDK (6762) +5.2%

### 🥈 2nd: AI Semiconductor (+7.1%)

Broad gains across AI chip-related stocks following NVIDIA's strong results. Advantest (6857) surged +9.2%, leading the theme on expanding HBM (High Bandwidth Memory) test demand. FY2026 orders remain robust. Socionext (6526) announced new automotive AI chip orders and gained +6.3%. The AI Semiconductor theme is up +29.2% on the month — the best-performing theme overall.

**Key stocks**: Advantest (6857) +9.2%, Socionext (6526) +6.3%, Renesas (6723) +4.8%

### 🥉 3rd: Semiconductor Equipment (+5.8%)

Tokyo Electron (8035) led gains as the sub-2nm fabrication race accelerates capex spending. Order backlogs remain at record levels. SCREEN Holdings (7735) gained +7.1% on rising cleaning equipment demand. ASML's expanding EUV order book provides a further tailwind.

**Key stocks**: Tokyo Electron (8035) +5.3%, SCREEN HD (7735) +7.1%, Disco (6146) +4.9%

### 4th: Memory (+5.3%)

DRAM price increases driven by HBM demand expansion continue. Kioxia HD (285A) surged +6.8% on improving NAND supply-demand dynamics. The Memory theme is up +26.3% on the month, ranking among the top semiconductor-adjacent plays.

### 5th: Wearables (+4.9%)

Anticipation of Apple WWDC 2026 (Jun 9–) drove speculative buying in Apple Watch and VisionPro supply chain names. Sony Group (6758) gained +3.8% on strong sensor division performance. The theme is up +26.7% monthly.

---

## Top Falling Themes

### 📉 1st: Shipbuilding (-5.1%)

Profit-taking continued as order backlogs are perceived to have peaked. Kawasaki Heavy Industries (7012) fell -6.3%, and Mitsui E&S (7003) declined -4.2%. The theme is down -16.3% monthly. However, defense-related shipbuilding demand (escorts, patrol vessels) remains solid, making stock selection within the theme crucial.

### 📉 2nd: Oil & Gas (-3.2%)

OPEC+ phased production increase reports weighed on crude prices. INPEX (1605) fell -3.8%, Idemitsu Kosan (5019) declined -2.9%. WTI crude dropped to the low $70/bbl range, weighing on the broader energy sector.

### 📉 3rd: Solar Power (-2.8%)

Renewable energy policy uncertainty kept sellers in control. West Holdings (1407) fell -3.5%. The long-term decarbonization trend remains intact, however.

---

## Macro Environment & Market Signals

| Indicator | This Week | WoW | Signal |
|-----------|-----------|-----|--------|
| Nikkei 225 | 39,180 | +2.3% | Solid — uptrend continues |
| TOPIX | 2,785 | +1.8% | Slightly lagging Nikkei |
| S&P 500 | 5,420 | +1.9% | AI-led strength |
| USD/JPY | 156.2 | +0.5% | Yen weak — tailwind for exporters |
| QQQ | — | +3.1% | Tech-led risk-on |
| VIX | 14.2 | -1.3pt | Low vol — bullish sentiment |

**Market assessment**: Risk-on environment persists. VIX remains in the low-14s, indicating sustained capital inflows into equities. Yen weakness supports export and global themes, but the 155+ level is near BOJ intervention watch territory.

---

## Key Points for Next Week

1. **US CPI release (Jun 11)**: May inflation data. A beat would dent rate-cut expectations and weigh on growth stocks; a miss would boost tech/semiconductor themes
2. **Apple WWDC 2026 (Jun 9–13)**: Apple Intelligence 2.0 and new VisionPro expected — major catalyst for AI and Wearables themes
3. **BOJ meeting minutes**: Watch for additional rate hike timing hints — significant for Banking/Finance theme
4. **MLCC volume sustainability**: Two consecutive weeks at #1 — volume persistence is key to continued trend

---

## Investment Strategy

**Theme momentum strategy**: Semiconductor/AI momentum has accelerated for 4 consecutive weeks, making trend-following the most effective approach. MLCC / Electronic Components in particular shows volume surge consistent with institutional entry.

**Risk management**: Resource themes (Shipbuilding, Oil) are in correction. Consider reducing positions in these themes and shifting capital toward Semiconductor/AI plays.

**Next week scenarios**: US CPI will be decisive. If in line with expectations, the semiconductor/AI uptrend likely accelerates. If higher than expected, it could trigger a growth-to-value rotation. Monitor USD/JPY within the 155–157 range for position management.
